output.prompt:
Create the feature context document at .sdd/specs/001-auth/context.md for feat-auth.

You MUST read these files before writing context:
- DATABASE.md
- AGENTS.md
- PROJECT_AGENTS.md

Use the OCP project rules from those files as binding constraints.
Context document MUST use these sections exactly:
1. PROBLEM STATEMENT
2. DOMAIN KNOWLEDGE
3. STAKEHOLDERS
4. CONSTRAINTS
5. ASSUMPTIONS
6. OPEN QUESTIONS

Feature scope:
Build the OCP authentication feature with local email/password auth, Google OAuth login, JWT httpOnly cookie session, bcrypt password hashing, email verification, password reset, profile access, role-aware backend authorization, blocked/deleted user rejection, and frontend withCredentials support. Backend remains the source of truth. Do not use Bearer auth, localStorage JWT, frontend role authority, or raw OAuth token storage.

Include these auth constraints:
- JWT is stored only in httpOnly Cookie.
- Backend reads auth token from req.cookies.
- Frontend sends credentials with withCredentials: true.
- Local auth uses bcrypt and users.password_hash.
- Google OAuth maps users through oauth_accounts.provider = GOOGLE and provider_user_id = Google sub.
- Do not store Google id_token, access_token, or refresh_token in database or logs.
- Do not trust frontend-sent email, role, providerUserId, amount, payment status, or course access.
- User blocked/deleted status must reject local and Google login.
- Payment provider is separate from identity provider: payments.provider = VNPAY, oauth_accounts.provider = GOOGLE.

Do not implement code. Only create or update context.md.

input.args:
SPECIFY_FEATURE_DIRECTORY=.sdd/specs/001-auth

Use the latest Speckit command semantics for speckit.specify.

You MUST read these files before generating spec.md:
- DATABASE.md
- AGENTS.md
- PROJECT_AGENTS.md
- .sdd/specs/001-auth/context.md

Generate the feature specification for feat-auth.

Feature scope:
Build the OCP authentication feature with local email/password auth, Google OAuth login, JWT httpOnly cookie session, bcrypt password hashing, email verification, password reset, profile access, role-aware backend authorization, blocked/deleted user rejection, and frontend withCredentials support. Backend remains the source of truth. Do not use Bearer auth, localStorage JWT, frontend role authority, or raw OAuth token storage.

Required behavior:
- Register learner with email/password.
- Login/logout using JWT in httpOnly Cookie.
- Read current user/profile from backend session.
- Verify email and reset password using hashed one-time tokens.
- Support Google OAuth login/linking using oauth_accounts.
- Reject blocked/deleted users for all login methods.
- Enforce backend source of truth for role/userId/access.
- Keep auth module boundaries; other modules consume Auth/User contracts.
- Define out-of-scope explicitly: payment checkout, enrollment, learning progress, admin CRUD, course CRUD.

Resolved product decisions:
- Pending-verification local users MUST NOT receive a login session until email verification succeeds.
- Google OAuth users with backend-verified Google email may be created as email_verified active learner accounts.
- If a backend-verified Google email matches an existing OCP user email but no oauth_accounts link exists, reject Google login with a safe GOOGLE_ACCOUNT_CONFLICT error and require the user to sign in locally before linking Google.
- Refresh-token-backed sessions are in scope. Store only refresh token hashes in refresh_tokens; rotate/revoke refresh tokens on refresh/logout.
- Cookie names: ocp_access_token and ocp_refresh_token.
- Middleware attaches trusted identity to req.user.
- Verification token expiry: 24 hours. Password reset token expiry: 30 minutes. Refresh token expiry: 7 days. Access token expiry: 15 minutes.

Must not include implementation details beyond what Speckit spec requires.

Use the latest Speckit command semantics for speckit.plan.

You MUST read these files before generating plan.md:
- DATABASE.md
- AGENTS.md
- PROJECT_AGENTS.md
- .sdd/specs/001-auth/context.md
- .sdd/specs/001-auth/spec.md

Technical constraints:
- Backend: NodeJS Express-style REST API.
- ORM/database: Prisma + MySQL.
- Validation: Zod for body/query/params.
- Auth: JWT httpOnly Cookie, bcrypt, Google OAuth.
- Data model must align with users, refresh_tokens, email_verifications, password_reset_tokens, oauth_accounts.
- Controllers must not contain business logic.
- Services must not import Prisma directly.
- Repositories own database access.
- Do not store raw JWT, raw reset/verification tokens, raw Google tokens, password plain text, or secrets.
- Use backend contracts for identity/session; frontend guards are UX only.

Use the latest Speckit command semantics for speckit.plan.

- DATABASE.md

Generate atomic, dependency-ordered, testable tasks for feat-auth.
Each task must reference spec requirements and include clear done criteria.
Include tests for local auth, Google OAuth, cookie behavior, validation errors, blocked/deleted user rejection, duplicate email/account linking policy, and secret/token leakage prevention.
Do not include implementation execution in this workflow.
